title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: Four Wheels and a Dream: Skateboarding Goes Olympic
content.text: When skateboarding made its Olympic debut at the Tokyo Games in 2021, many in the traditional sports establishment were surprised by its inclusion. Yet for the millions who have skated streets, parks and concrete bowls for decades, the moment felt long overdue. (37) [37] El camino hacia los Juegos fue largo y polémico. [/37] The journey from subculture to Olympic podium had taken more than fifty years and was never without controversy. || Skateboarding was born on the pavements of California in the late 1950s, when surfers attached roller-skate wheels to wooden planks to practise their moves on dry land. (38) [38] Desde sus orígenes, el deporte fue sinónimo de libertad. [/38] The sport thrived on improvisation and a deliberate rejection of authority. Skaters created their own vocabulary, their own fashion and their own spaces — often illegally — in carparks and empty swimming pools. || The International Olympic Committee first approached the skateboarding community about inclusion in the 1990s, but negotiations collapsed over questions of governance and judging. Who would have authority over the sport? How would tricks be scored? (39) [39] Las negociaciones sobre la gobernanza tardaron décadas. [/39] It was not until 2016, when the IOC confirmed skateboarding's place at Tokyo 2020, that a framework was finally agreed. || The Olympic format introduced two disciplines: street and park. Street skateboarding takes place on a course designed to resemble an urban environment, with stairs, rails and ledges. (40) [40] La modalidad de parque se desarrolla en un cuenco cóncavo. [/40] Park, by contrast, is skated in a deep concrete bowl, rewarding riders who generate speed and perform aerial manoeuvres high above the rim. || The youngest competitors at Tokyo were thirteen and fourteen years old. Critics argued that such young athletes faced undue pressure. (41) [41] Otros veían en la juventud de los atletas una muestra del espíritu del deporte. [/41] Supporters countered that skateboarding had always been a young person's discipline, and that early participation was simply part of its culture. || Since Tokyo, debates about skateboarding's Olympic future have continued. Some veteran skaters worry that commercialisation will erode the sport's independent spirit. (42) [42] Sin embargo, muchos participantes jóvenes consideran que los Juegos amplían las oportunidades. [/42] For them, the Olympic stage is not a threat to skateboarding's identity but an invitation to share it with the world.

paragraphs.A: From its earliest days, it carried an ethos of freedom and self-expression that set it apart from conventional athletics.
paragraphs.B: Others, however, see the Games as opening doors to funding, facilities and recognition that the sport has long deserved.
paragraphs.C: Those disagreements persisted through several Olympic cycles before a resolution was found.
paragraphs.D: Riders perform runs and tricks that are judged on difficulty, execution and style.
paragraphs.E: For many long-time practitioners, it represented the ultimate validation of a discipline they had devoted their lives to.
paragraphs.F: To them, it simply proved that skateboarding had always belonged to the young.
paragraphs.G: The course is designed to test technical precision on a flat, obstacle-filled surface.

questions.explanation: E connects the feeling of validation of veteran skaters with the long history described below.
A expands the Californian origin with the spirit of freedom and expression that characterised the sport from the beginning.
C summarises the prolonged disagreements before the 2016 resolution mentioned in the following sentence.
G describes the street format before contrasting with the park modality in the following sentence.
F introduces the positive perspective on the youth of the athletes that supporters defend below.
B represents the vision of young participants who see the Games as an opportunity, contrasting with the concerns of veterans.
